microsoft_ft: Owned the **infrastructure platform roadmap** for Azure's resilience validation system, defining API contracts, self-service scheduling primitives, and deployment models that scaled the platform to support **45+ annual drills** across enterprise customers.

Led 0→1 development of an internal **Resilience Automation Platform**, writing PRDs and self-service capabilities that reduced operational toil by 30% and shifted execution from a manual 2-person model to a scalable, multi-tenant system.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive rack-level resilience testing capability, delivering the **0→1 drill program in 4 months** with a 94% recovery rate — surfacing critical hardware defects and establishing a new continuous validation primitive.

Served as bridge PM on a sovereign-cloud **network isolation test tied to a $1.5B+ contract**, running technical reviews with engineering and GTM, translating architectural tradeoffs into deal-ready narratives for CTO-level stakeholders.

Drove cross-org roadmap prioritization across 14 enterprise deal cycles — separating one-off customer asks from real **platform investments** and writing competitive teardowns and GTM primers used in active sales motions.
Scaled team leverage via an internal **AI agent for drill planning**, restructuring workflows to cut planning cycle time by 39% and increase drill capacity by 21% without adding headcount.
microsoft_2023: Championed **developer experience** improvements for AI-driven code generation workflows, running user demos and training that drove adoption across 14 Azure engineering teams and saved **37 engineering hours** monthly.

Shaped the infrastructure product roadmap by conducting **11+ technical interviews** with Azure service teams, surfacing critical API and tooling gaps that directly informed the intent-based YAML generation feature prioritization.

Improved **platform data integrity** by enforcing metadata standards and migrating documentation to an AI-powered semantic search system, reducing developer lookup time by 83% across internal engineering consumers.
microsoft_2022: Secured cross-functional alignment across **140+ engineering teams** on a unified automation prioritization framework, accelerating Azure region launches by 28% and generating **$3M in accelerated revenue**.

Defined a platform strategy to eliminate operational toil in region launches — conducting discovery with 20+ service teams, quantifying **81 hours of manual effort** per region, and building the roadmap to automate critical deployment paths.

Built a **Power BI developer dashboard** to surface automation gaps and toil distribution across 140+ teams, enabling leadership to prioritize high-ROI infrastructure investments and deprecate low-value manual workflows.
amazon_robotics: Defined the **legacy OS migration strategy** for a 2,000+ unit robotics fleet, mapping SDK and system dependencies across 1,200+ stations to achieve zero operational downtime during the pilot transition.

Prioritized the engineering backlog across Agile sprint cycles, resolving **40+ high-priority developer intake tickets** and stabilizing the internal SDK tooling process used by cross-functional robotics engineering teams.
Aligned IT, Operations, and Engineering on a **CI/CD pipeline rollout**, translating infrastructure tradeoffs into a shared deployment model that accelerated the software release cycle by 25%.

Authored deployment runbooks and deprecation comms for the OS migration, ensuring internal engineering teams had clear, structured documentation to execute the transition with **zero production incidents**.
pro_painters: Increased **job bookings by 26%** by owning the end-to-end CRM platform rollout — standardizing the scoping, proposal, and invoicing lifecycle across 200+ monthly customer engagements.

Reduced **customer acquisition cost by 13%** and lifted conversions by 2.7% by executing a digital-first GTM strategy, optimizing web and search surfaces to improve top-of-funnel developer and consumer reach.

Conducted financial analysis across **1,000+ project records** to identify margin leaks and rebuild leadership pricing models, delivering a profitability framework that improved deal-level unit economics.
